next_steps: Send serum for Leptospira PCR and microscopic agglutination test (MAT)
Perform peripheral blood smear to evaluate for Babesia organisms
Send serum for Babesia microti PCR and serology
Send blood/respiratory samples for broad-spectrum infectious disease molecular testing
Monitor renal function and consider hemodialysis for severe azotemia
Continue doxycycline as it covers Leptospirosis and Babesiosis (when combined with other agents)
Perform peripheral blood smear for intraerythrocytic organisms (Babesia/Malaria)
Send serum for Leptospira PCR and microscopic agglutination test (MAT)
Order hemolysis workup (LDH, haptoglobin, reticulocyte count, peripheral smear review)
Send urine Legionella antigen and respiratory viral/atypical pathogen PCR panel
Consult Nephrology for assessment of acute renal failure and potential initiation of hemodialysis
Evaluate coagulation profile (PT, PTT, Fibrinogen, D-dimer) for disseminated intravascular coagulation
Review infectious disease consult recommendations for escalation to atovaquone/azithromycin if Babesiosis is suspected
Repeat blood cultures and consider echocardiography to rule out infective endocarditis
